What we help with › Stress & anxiety
For all • All ages • Every stage of life

Stress & anxiety

Stress is not just in your head. It lives in the body: tight shoulders, a clenched jaw, broken sleep, a system that never quite switches off. We help people of every age and walk of life calm that system down, gently and without judgement.

Understanding it · unique copy per page: what it feels like, why it happens in plain terms, reassurance · MUST include the areas-and-causes passage naming the joints, tissues (muscle, fascia, tendon, ligament) and causes (sport, accident, trauma, fall, gradual onset, underlying condition) relevant to this theme; this is the SEO body-coverage layer · sidebar stays verbatim
Understanding it

How stress shows up in the body, in plain terms

When life asks too much for too long, the body's alarm system stays switched on. Muscles brace as if for impact, breathing turns shallow, sleep gets lighter, and everything aches and irritates more than it should. None of this is weakness and none of it is imagined: it is a protective system doing its job too well, and like any system stuck on high alert, it can be helped to settle.

We see stress and anxiety in the body every day: tight necks and shoulders, jaw clenching and grinding, tension headaches, a tight chest and shallow breathing, an unsettled stomach, restless nights, flat energy, and aches that flare exactly when life is hardest. Sometimes there is a clear cause (work pressure, caring for others, illness, pain or loss) and sometimes there is no single reason at all. We look at how your body is holding it: breathing, posture, muscle guarding, sleep and energy, and shape care around calming the system: unhurried hands-on treatment, simple breathing and movement you can actually use, and realistic changes at your pace. We work alongside your GP or any other support you have, never instead of it.

Get in touch

The easiest first step is a conversation. Tell us what is going on and we will point you the right way, with no pressure at all.

Call 07773 814488

What to expect

From first call to feeling better

What is likely going on

A body stuck on high alert is a protective system overworking, not a character flaw. Once you can feel what it is doing, it becomes something that can be worked with.

How we assess it

An unhurried conversation, in confidence and without judgement, and a gentle look at how your body is carrying the load: breathing, tension, sleep and energy.

What getting better looks like

Progress tends to arrive quietly: deeper sleep, shoulders that sit lower, more room to breathe. Simple tools you keep, and a plan that flexes with your life.

Veiled photo band · 70% navy wash, homepage CTA treatment, at most once per page · requires this page's photo appended as the .veil CSS rule (see marked slot in the stylesheet) · run the photo overlay simulation before sign-off What it means for you

Room to breathe again

An evening without the racing mind, a night of proper sleep, shoulders that finally drop when you exhale. Calm is not gone for good; the body can be shown its way back.

The right care for this · NEW condition-page element, the page's routing job per the IA · promote the one or two services this condition routes to, benefit-led, with direct links · keep to two cards maximum
The right care for this

Where this usually leads

Performance & Wellbeing

Reassurance · approved copy, reused verbatim from Our Approach · safe, gentle, at your pace Reassurance

Safe, gentle, and at your pace

Everything is done with care and at a pace that suits you, and nothing is rushed or forced. The aim throughout is simple: that you feel in good hands, understood, and steadily better.

Recognised and accountable

Held to recognised professional standards

Members and registrants of the Royal Society of Medicine, the Complementary and Natural Healthcare Council, the Institute for Functional Medicine, the Society of Sports Therapists, the Institute of Sport and Remedial Massage and the British Medical Acupuncture Society, among others.
